nativeRange.habitat: Padina species inhabit shallow reef and rocky subtidal zones from the intertidal to subtidal, attaching to hard substrate or growing as epiphytes on larger seaweeds.

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that graze heavily on macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish in small tanks)

imageCandidates.notes: Padina pavonica by Holger Krisp, 3000x2161. Tack-sharp textbook peacock's-tail scroll/funnel form with concentric banding and whitish calcareous zones; cleanest, highest-res shot in the pool.
Padina boryana by Wynand Uys, 761x685. Sharp in-situ cluster showing the diagnostic inrolled scroll fronds, strong concentric banding and silvery-iridescent calcareous surface.
Padina sanctae-crucis by Damien Wallace, 1024x771. Vibrant, sharp in-water group of golden banded fans on reef rock; good genus-representative colour and form.
Padina durvillei by Michael Bakker Paiva, 1024x768. Sharp in-situ clump of full ruffled olive-gold fronds, whole thallus in frame; adds a distinct species to the genus set.
Padina tetrastromatica by Srichakra Pranav Tamarapalli, 1024x473. Tidepool cluster of banded fronds; fifth distinct Padina species for genus breadth (slightly lower resolution).

summary: The Blue Scroll is a specialty brown macroalga forming thin, fan- to scroll-shaped fronds with concentric banding. Under full-spectrum reef lighting its fronds display a blue iridescent shimmer — a structural coloration effect that fades to brown under dimmer or narrow light spectra. It deposits a thin calcium carbonate layer on its fronds and is best suited to display reef tanks.
careNotes: The Blue Scroll (Padina sp.) is a specialty ornamental brown macroalga in the family Dictyotaceae, order Dictyotales — one of the very few calcareous members of the brown algae. Its thallus grows as a thin, fan- or scroll-shaped frond with characteristic concentric banding and marginal hair zones, typical of the Padina genus. The distinctive blue iridescent shimmer is a structural coloration effect visible under full-spectrum aquarium lighting; the alga tends to appear predominantly brown under lower or narrow-spectrum illumination. The species-level identification is not resolved from available trade listings (sold as Padina sp.); the Padina genus encompasses numerous tropical and subtropical marine species distributed widely across the Indo-Pacific and Atlantic. Standard reef water parameters are generally advised: temperature around 24–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, and specific gravity 1.023–1.026. Medium to strong lighting is recommended to elicit the characteristic blue shimmer; inadequate illumination tends to suppress the iridescence. Growth rate and nutrient uptake values for this species (medium and low, respectively) are extrapolated from family-level care standards for Dictyotaceae, as species-specific captive data is limited in the hobby literature. Propagation in the aquarium is typically achieved by fragmentation of healthy frond sections. As a display specimen, placement on exposed rockwork in moderate flow is generally recommended. Note that Padina is lightly calcareous — it deposits a thin layer of calcium carbonate on its fronds, which may contribute minimally to alkalinity consumption in the tank. IMPORTANT: as a brown alga, the Blue Scroll will MELT at the base if buried in sand — it must be attached to hard substrate via superglue, zip-tie, fishing line, or wedging among rockwork. This is a specialty item with limited availability compared to more commonly traded macroalgae genera.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by fragmentation — breaking or cutting healthy frond sections and attaching them to rockwork via superglue, zip-tie, or fishing line, or by wedging them into crevices. Sexual reproduction, which in brown algae follows an alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle, has not been documented in hobby aquaria for this species.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty form. Product listing for 'Blue Scroll Algae' (Padina sp.). Provides identification, imagery, morphology description (blue shimmer under full-spectrum lighting, brown macroalga with scroll form), and general reef compatibility context.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Padina (alga)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic classification (Dictyotaceae / Dictyotales / Phaeophyceae), morphology confirmation (fan-shaped fronds, concentric banding, lightly calcareous), and native range (tropical to temperate, Indo-Pacific to Atlantic, intertidal to ~30 m typical photic zone). No aquarium husbandry parameters provided.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
